question:
Q1. Two Sum

Given an array of integers and a target value, return the indices of two numbers such that they add up to the target.

Q2. Maximum Subarray Sum

Given an integer array, find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return its sum.

Q3. Leaders in Array

Given an array, find all elements that are greater than all elements to their right.

Q4. Valid Parentheses

Given a string containing only ‘( ) { } [ ]’, determine if the string is valid (balanced).

Q5. Rotate Array by K

Given an array, rotate it to the right by k steps.

Q6. Longest Common Prefix

Given an array of strings, find the longest common prefix string.

Q7. Missing Number

Given an array containing n distinct numbers from 0 to n, find the one number missing.

Q8. Palindrome Check

Given a string, check if it is a palindrome.

Q9. Count Vowels

Given a string, count the number of vowels present.

Q10. Minimum Platforms

Given arrival and departure times of trains, find the minimum number of platforms required.

Q11. Frequency of Elements

Given an array, print the frequency of each element.

Q12. Second Largest Element

Find the second largest element in an array.

Q13. Remove Duplicates (Sorted Array)

Remove duplicates from a sorted array and return the new length.

Q14. Anagram Check

Given two strings, check whether they are anagrams.

Q15. First Non-Repeating Character

Find the first character in a string that does not repeat.
answer:
H1.

Think about storing elements you have already seen. For each element, check if its required pair already exists.

H2.

If the running sum becomes negative, it won’t help in future—restart from next element.

H3.

Traverse the array from the end and keep track of the maximum seen so far.

H4.

Use a data structure that follows Last-In-First-Out. Match closing brackets with the most recent opening.

H5.

Avoid shifting elements repeatedly. Instead, reverse parts of the array in steps.

H6.

Sorting the array of strings can simplify comparison. Only compare the first and last strings.

H7.

Use the mathematical sum formula or XOR approach to find the missing value.

H8.

Use two pointers: one from the beginning and one from the end, and compare characters.

H9.

Check each character against a predefined set of vowels. Don’t forget uppercase letters.

H10.

Sort both arrival and departure arrays. Use two pointers to simulate platform usage.

H11.

Use a hashmap/dictionary to count occurrences efficiently.

H12.

Keep track of the largest and second largest values in a single traversal.

H13.

Use two pointers—one to track unique elements, another to iterate through the array.

H14.

Compare character counts of both strings or sort them and compare results.

H15.

First store frequency of each character, then find the first one with count = 1.
